message:
feat(redact-memo): content-addressed memoization for ingestion redaction [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34]

ibuuh.34 lexical-side wiring: redaction is a pure, regex-heavy
transformation that runs against every persisted message content +
metadata blob. Salvage replays, repeated assistant boilerplate, and
historical re-ingest all feed identical content through the regex
engine over and over. This commit adds a ContentAddressedMemoCache
wrapper that lets callers stop re-running the regex on repeated
content while a SECRET_PATTERNS bump invalidates every prior entry
transparently.

What landed:
- redaction_algorithm_fingerprint() -> "redact-v1:<blake3-hex>"
  - Hash covers every pattern source string concatenated with NUL +
    the REDACTED placeholder, so any tightening of a regex (or change
    to the placeholder) bumps the fingerprint and invalidates cached
    entries with no manual intervention.
  - Computed once via Lazy and reused process-wide.
- pub(crate) struct MemoizingRedactor wrapping
  ContentAddressedMemoCache<String>:
  - DEFAULT_CAPACITY = 4096 (covers a few thousand distinct message
    bodies before LRU eviction).
  - redact_text(input) hashes input with blake3 (fixed-width key,
    no pathological 1-MiB-content cache keys), looks up under
    (content_hash, "redact_text", algorithm_fingerprint), runs the
    legacy regex on miss, inserts result.
  - redact_json(value) recurses object/array, memoizes every string
    scalar AND every object key independently.
  - Empty-input fast-path: skips cache entirely (zero-length inputs
    don't burn cache slots during ingestion of system messages).
  - stats() exposes hit/miss/insert counters from the underlying
    ContentAddressedMemoCache for operator audit.

Equivalence + reuse + invalidation gates (5 new tests):
- memoizing_redactor_matches_uncached_for_arbitrary_input pins
  byte-for-byte agreement with redact_text across clean / single-
  secret / multi-secret / empty / 10-KiB inputs, including BOTH the
  first call and the second call (cache-hit invariance).
- memoizing_redactor_reuses_cache_for_repeated_content pins
  hits=2 / misses=1 / inserts=1 after three identical calls.
- memoizing_redactor_keys_isolate_by_algorithm_fingerprint pins the
  fingerprint structure ("redact-v1:<64-char hex>") so a regression
  in either the version epoch or the hash format trips a clear
  assertion.
- memoizing_redactor_redact_json_matches_uncached_for_nested_shapes
  pins recursive JSON traversal byte-for-byte.
- memoizing_redactor_empty_input_skips_cache pins the empty-input
  fast-path so ingestion runs with thousands of empty system
  messages don't churn the cache.

22/22 src/indexer/redact_secrets.rs lib tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib indexer::redact_secrets.

Note on ibuuh.32: not closed despite three sinks shipped with
equivalence gates. The primary refresh path callers in
persist_conversations_batched_inner (indexer/mod.rs:13915, 13943,
13951, 15970, 15987) still call legacy map_to_internal +
add_messages_with_conversation_id; both files are BlueRabbit-locked.
ibuuh.32 needs that wiring before it can close cleanly per AC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
